Travel Journal / Inner Compass

Now Roaming

It's good to be lost

A record of movement, grit, wonder, and the quiet work of becoming clear. Follow the routes, rituals, meals, storms, strangers, and thresholds that shape my life in motion.

Read my Journal Trace the Path
4 Open routes
42 Field notes
01 Inner path

Latest from the road

Dispatches from humid mornings, night markets, ferry decks, mountain temples, and the places where experience starts acting like a teacher.

Stop 1: Korea 6,900 miles from home

What did I do?

A first entry on surrendering the schedule, following incense through back streets, and finding a quieter compass in the storm.
Panama Coast

Black sand, bright fruit, old questions

What a volcanic beach taught me about choosing fewer things and feeling them all the way through.
Oaxaca Food

The market is a monastery if you listen

Smoke, masa, chile, and the kind of presence that arrives when you stop trying to optimize the day.
Madeira Climb

Above the clouds, below the ego

A steep path, a wet jacket, and the useful humiliation of realizing the mountain is not impressed.

Route Theory

Every trip leaves a mark

Now Roaming tracks more than locations. Each route is a sequence of thresholds: the place you arrived, the habit you lost, the lesson that followed you home.

The map stays loose on purpose. The point is not collecting pins. The point is noticing the shape of the person moving between them.

Arrival Land with open hands.
Friction Let discomfort name the lesson.
Clarity Carry less. See more.

Field notes for the path

Short observations from the edge of movement: the practices, objects, and decisions that make roaming feel grounded.

01

Travel light, pay attention

The best gear disappears. The best memories get sharper when there is space to receive them.

02

Eat where the smoke is

A line outside a tiny stall is usually better guidance than a hundred polished recommendations.

03

Leave room for silence

Not every view needs a caption. Some places do their work after the camera goes down.

The Philosophy

Experience as a discipline

Now Roaming is built on the idea that travel can be more than escape. It can be a practice: physical movement in service of clearer perception, better questions, deeper courage, and a little less noise between you and the life you actually mean to live.

Dispatches

Get the next route

Monthly notes from the road: essays, itineraries, hard-won travel lessons, and the inner weather behind the outer miles.

Now Roaming you are now roaming Travel / Experience / Enlightenment
